evals.prompt: I'm starting a new React app (Next.js) for a project management tool. It has projects, tasks, team members, and a dashboard. Users can filter, sort, and search tasks. There's real-time collaboration. Help me design the frontend architecture.
evals.expected_output: Should design component hierarchy, choose state management strategy (server state vs client state), define data fetching patterns, propose folder structure, and address real-time needs.
assertions: Decomposes UI into page, feature, UI, and layout component categories
Separates server state (TanStack Query) from client state (useState/Zustand)
Addresses real-time data with WebSocket + cache invalidation strategy
Defines design tokens for visual consistency
Addresses code splitting at route level
Error boundary strategy at feature boundaries
URL state for filters, sort, and search (shareable links)

evals.prompt: Our React app has grown to 200+ components and state management is a mess — we have Redux, Context, local state, and even some global variables. Some components re-render 50+ times when you type in a search box. How do we untangle this?
evals.expected_output: Should diagnose the root causes (mixed state paradigms, no separation of server vs client state, over-connected Redux), and propose an incremental migration strategy.
assertions: Diagnoses the re-render problem as likely unnecessary Redux subscriptions
Recommends migrating server data OUT of Redux into TanStack Query / SWR
Recommends keeping Redux only for genuinely global client state
Suggests an incremental migration (one feature at a time, not big-bang)
Applies the state placement ladder (useState first, then up)
Does NOT recommend adding yet another state management library on top
Addresses the performance issue specifically (memoization, selector optimization)
